title: Developer data-handling training — prod-access engineers lacking training
exclusions: Does NOT cover all engineering staff — focuses on those with production access to regulated data.
evidence: Saved search 'dev_data_handling_training' (weekly) intersects IdP prod-access groups against LMS DPO-approved course completions.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.32(4) requires awareness of personnel processing personal data.
HIPAA workforce training applies to workforce with ePHI access.
A.6.3 information-security awareness, education, and training.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.32(4) (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Engineer bob is in the prod-access group for customer-database but has no current DPO course record; the UC fires the next week.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All prod-access engineers have current training; no alert.

dataSources: IdP prod-access group membership, LMS completion records for DPO-approved course ids.
spl:
| rest /services/identity/prod-access-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group scope
| where scope="regulated"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=data_handling_v* earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user group scope last_completion
description: Prevents the 'developers with production access but stale training' anti-pattern that surfaces in GDPR Art.32 enforcement cases.
value: Links IdP posture to training posture — the evidence auditors actually ask for.
implementation: (1) Tag IdP groups for prod access with regulated scope; (2) publish DPO-approved course IDs; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-revoke access after configured overdue window.
visualization: Table of engineers without training, bar chart by team, single-value 'prod-access engineers with current training'.
knownFalsePositives: Freshly-onboarded engineers in the 30-day grace window should be allow-listed by onboarding_date.
